title: Pisto Manchego
subtitle: Spanish ratatouille of courgettes, peppers, and tomatoes
excerpt: Pisto manchego is a Spanish vegetable stew of courgettes, peppers, tomatoes, and onion cooked slowly in olive oil until very soft and concentrated. It is the Spanish version of ratatouille. Serve warm with eggs fried on top, as a side dish, or at room temperature as a tapa.
sourceNotes: Pisto manchego originates in La Mancha in central Spain. It is one of a family of Mediterranean slow-cooked vegetable dishes that includes the French ratatouille and the Italian peperonata. The dish is traditionally served with fried eggs on top or alongside cured meats.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze in portions once cooled. Keeps for 3 months.
recipe.batchNotes: Makes well in a large batch. Keeps in the fridge for 5 days.

content.text: Pisto manchego is a Spanish slow-cooked vegetable stew of courgettes, peppers, onion, and tomatoes in olive oil. The vegetables are cooked separately in stages to concentrate each one before being combined and simmered down together. The result is soft, sweet, and deeply flavoured. It improves the next day.
Heat half the olive oil in a large casserole over medium heat. Add the onions and cook for 10 minutes until soft and starting to colour. Add the garlic and cook for 2 minutes. Transfer to a plate.
Add the remaining oil to the pan. Cook the peppers for 8 minutes over medium heat until soft. Add the courgette and cook for 5 minutes more.
Return the onion mixture to the pan. Add the chopped tomatoes and smoked paprika if using. Season with salt and pepper. Stir to combine.

Simmer gently uncovered for 25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are very soft and most of the liquid has evaporated. The pisto should be thick and concentrated, not watery. Taste and adjust the seasoning.

items.cause: Not cooked down long enough, or vegetables too wet
items.fix: Increase the heat slightly and cook uncovered for a further 15 minutes, stirring frequently. The pisto should have the consistency of a thick sauce, not a soup.

items.symptom: The courgette has broken down completely
items.fix: Add the courgette later in the process if you prefer more texture. The traditional version has everything very soft.

content.text: Pisto with fried eggs: make two indentations in the finished pisto, crack an egg into each, cover with a lid, and cook over gentle heat for 3 minutes until the whites are just set. Serve directly from the pan.

Pisto is a summer dish from the interior of Spain, made when courgettes and peppers are abundant. It is eaten as a tapa at room temperature, as a side dish alongside pork chops or grilled fish, or as a light meal with eggs. Cold from the fridge the next day it makes a good base for a sandwich.
